title: Prometheus の 503 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article accurately identifies the primary causes of Prometheus 503 errors.
Troubleshooting steps are practical and include relevant command-line examples.
Direct references to official Prometheus documentation are provided, enhancing credibility.
absolute wording may overstate technical certainty

sources.title: Storage - Prometheus
sources.claim: --storage.tsdb.path: Where Prometheus writes its database. Defaults to data/.

sources.title: Configuration - Prometheus
sources.claim: A configuration reload is triggered by sending a SIGHUP to the Prometheus process or sending a HTTP POST request to the /-/reload endpoint (when the --web.enable-lifecycle flag is enabled).

sources.title: Management API - Prometheus
sources.claim: Health check GET /-/healthy HEAD /-/healthy This endpoint always returns 200 and should be used to check Prometheus health. This endpoint triggers a reload of the Prometheus configuration and rule files. It's disabled by default and can be enabled via the --web.enable-lifecycle flag.

sources.title: How to reload prometheus config without restarting? - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: To do this, you have to first start your Prometheus process with --web.enable-lifecycle flag. After starting with this flag, then you can hit the below URL every time you want to reload your config changes. curl -X POST <your_prometheus_server_url>:<port>/-/reload.

sources.title: Intro to PromTools - KodeKloud
sources.claim: One of the most useful commands provided by PromTools is “check config,” which verifies your Prometheus configuration file. This ensures that your setup is error-free before deployment, preventing potential downtime. For example, to validate your configuration, run: $ promtool check config /etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml

sources.title: promtool man | Linux Command Library
sources.claim: promtool is the command-line utility for Prometheus, the popular monitoring and alerting system. It validates configuration, tests rules, and queries metrics. Configuration checking catches syntax errors and invalid settings before applying changes.

sources.title: promtool - Prometheus
sources.claim: promtool check config Check if the config files are valid or not.

sources.title: Reloading Prometheus' Configuration - Robust Perception
sources.claim: There are two ways to ask Prometheus to reload it's configuration, a SIGHUP and the POSTing to the /-/reload handler. ... You can use the promtool command that comes with Prometheus to syntax check your configuration and rules.

sources.title: How do you manage Prometheus configuration changes? : r/devops - Reddit
sources.claim: A configuration reload is triggered by sending a SIGHUP to the Prometheus process or sending a HTTP POST request to the /-/reload endpoint (when the --web.enable-lifecycle flag is enabled).

sources.title: How to check my prometheus.yml has errors or not? - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: For that in windows type below command after navigating to Prometheus folder. promtool check config prometheus.yml. If there are no errors output will be... If you are using Ubuntu, and currently in different dictionary, type promtool check config /etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml.

sources.title: How to check your prometheus.yml is valid - Robust Perception
sources.claim: To facilitate this, promtool which comes with Prometheus has a check config command.

sources.title: Troubleshooting Managed Service for Prometheus | Google Cloud Observability
sources.claim: If you see one of the following errors, then the default service account on the node might be missing permissions: "Readiness probe failed: HTTP probe failed with statuscode: 503" ... "Context deadline exceeded" is a generic 503 error returned from Monarch for any ingestion-side problem that doesn't have a specific cause.

sources.title: Prometheus Container Hung on Creating 503 Error · Issue #3341 - GitHub
sources.claim: 503 is returned by prometheus when replaying its write ahead log. If it is very big it a) can take quite a while and b) could even exhaust resources causing crashloops. You are getting Out of Memory error! So you may need to increase the prometheus server's memory based on your load.

sources.title: intro-to-prometheus-breakouts/breakout-1-exploring-the-prometheus-ui.md at main - GitHub
sources.claim: The first one I would visit is the Targets page; this tells us what's been configured in the Prometheus server. These jobs are what we defined in the prometheus server yml config. They will either be up or down. Go to the Status menu and select Runtime & Build Information and review the configuration shown. The Configuration page from the Status menu will present a similar configuration we just went over, with a bit more targets added.

sources.title: Prometheus Troubleshooting Guide - Dynatrace Community
sources.claim: Troubleshooting often involves checking logs, verifying network connectivity, and inspecting the Prometheus UI.

sources.title: Why is Prometheus returning "HTTP status 503 Service Unavailable" when scraping my .NET Core microservice `/health_metrics` endpoint? - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: What could be causing Prometheus to return a 503 Service Unavailable error when the endpoint is accessible via a browser? Are there specific configurations in Prometheus or Docker that might need adjustment to resolve this issue? Could there be any resource constraints or timeout settings causing this behavior? Is there a way to get more detailed logging from Prometheus to diagnose the root cause of this issue?

sources.title: Prometheus starts and hangs, returns 503 · Issue #5814 - GitHub
sources.claim: Prometheus returns a 503 exit code. Moreover, top indicates that it slowly claims more memory until its used all the available memory on the underlying node.

sources.title: How to Debug Prometheus Scraping Issues | by Harold Finch - Medium
sources.claim: Target Down → Prometheus cannot reach the endpoint. Context Deadline Exceeded → Timeout during scrape.

sources.title: Prometheus storage - by Naresh Kancharla - Medium
sources.claim: Prometheus by default stores data to local tsdb.

sources.title: Troubleshoot Prometheus data source issues | Grafana documentation
sources.claim: Request timed out. Cause: The connection to Prometheus timed out before receiving a response.

sources.title: Troubleshoot Prometheus Issues | Verrazzano Enterprise Container Platform
sources.claim: If Prometheus storage reaches capacity, then metrics queries will no longer return results. Check the Prometheus logs. If there are messages indicating that the disk is full, then it will be necessary to either expand the storage or free disk space.

sources.title: Common Error Types - Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus - AWS Documentation
sources.claim: The service is temporarily unavailable. Try again later. HTTP Status Code: 503. ThrottlingException. Your request rate is too high. The AWS SDKs automatically retry requests that receive this exception.

sources.title: use the Prometheus `/-/healthy` endpoint for the default value for health_check_url · Issue #6966 - GitHub
sources.claim: by default, use the /-/healthy endpoint that Prometheus has, rather than the main prometheus root url. This is because sometimes Prometheus doesn't have the UI installed and this causes the root URL to tell the client to redirect to an invalid /graph endpoint, causing a error code to be returned, thus Kiali UI will report Prometheus being unreachable which isn't true.

sources.title: Flags - Prometheus
sources.claim: --storage.tsdb.path, Base path for metrics storage. Use with server mode only. data/.

sources.title: How to Debug Prometheus Scraping Issues | by Harold Finch - Medium
sources.claim: The /targets endpoint is a diagnostic treasure trove. Access it http://localhost:9090/targets. Statuses include. UP → Scrape successful; DOWN → Failure occurred.

sources.title: How to Configure Prometheus Scrape Targets - OneUptime
sources.claim: Check Prometheus UI for discovered targets at http://prometheus:9090/targets. Common issues include: Target shows as DOWN: Check network connectivity and firewall rules.

sources.title: Readiness probe failed: HTTP probe failed with statuscode: 503 - Google Groups
sources.claim: Having a problem with Prometheus never quite finishing starting up. and navigating to / returns a 502 Server Error. Readiness probe failed: HTTP probe failed with statuscode: 503.

sources.title: Target Down - kube-prometheus runbooks
sources.claim: The alert means that one or more prometheus scrape targets are down. It fires when at least 10% of scrape targets in a Service are unreachable. ... /targets page in Prometheus UI can be used to check the scrape error for the particular target. up == 0 query can be used to check the trend over time.

sources.title: Add `/-/healthy` and `/-/ready` endpoints · Issue #2831 · prometheus/prometheus - GitHub
sources.claim: Prometheus should provide an health endpoint which returns 200 if prometheus is ready to response to queries (recovery is done etc).

sources.title: 503 Error when connecting Prometheus Datasource - Grafana community forums
sources.claim: A Status: 503. Message: ReadObject: expect { or , or } or n, but found <, error found in #1 byte of …| <he|…, bigger context …| 503 Service Temporarily Unavai…

sources.title: Scrape with Prometheus operator result in random 503 · Issue #6929 - GitHub
sources.claim: Every time when we roll new nodes or when Postgres pods are recreated, we see 503 Error in Prometheus Target status for random Postgres pods. Solution: The issue was related to the size of the WAL directory. A 503 error can occur when Prometheus is replaying its write-ahead log, especially if it is very large.

sources.title: Getting 503 error from just one target among hundreds, a Prometheus issue?
sources.claim: The only way for this error to show up is if the target positively responded with a 503.

sources.title: Prometheus using more and more space without going down - Reddit
sources.claim: This morning, I got some datasource errors / 503. After looking, it seems the disk filled up.

sources.title: Prometheus: no space left on device | by Jackson Chen - Medium
sources.claim: Prometheus error messages ts=2024-03-12T05:17:54.625Z caller=scrape.go:1332 level=error component="scrape manager" scrape_pool=instance target=http://10.102.30.129:9182/metrics msg="Scrape commit failed" err="write to WAL: log samples: write /prometheus/wal/00007037: no space left on device". The Prometheus WAL (Write-Ahead-Log) directory stores pre-write logs used to persistently save the time-series data collected by the Prometheus Server. Error recovery: The WAL directory also provides error recovery capabilities. If Prometheus encounters errors while writing time-series data to persistent storage, it can recover data by re-reading the WAL directory.

sources.title: Troubleshoot Prometheus data source issues | Grafana documentation
sources.claim: If Grafana and Prometheus are running in separate containers, use the container IP address or hostname instead of localhost. For Grafana Cloud, ensure you have configured Private data source connect if your Prometheus instance is not publicly accessible.

sources.title: Troubleshooting | Prometheus
sources.claim: The article refers to '公式ドキュメントのTroubleshootingセクション'

sources.title: Management API | Prometheus
sources.claim: The article refers to '疎通確認ガイド' (connectivity confirmation guide)

sources.title: Suggest view target metrics in prometheus UI in proxy mode - Google Groups
sources.claim: In prometheus web UI under tab [Targets], we can see the targets and their urls.

sources.title: Viewing Monitored Targets - Prometheus Trainings by PromLabs
sources.claim: In case anything goes wrong during a scrape (whether the problem is a DNS resolution failure, a connection timeout, a bad metrics payload format, or any other reason), the target state will be shown as DOWN , along with an error message providing more detail about the scrape failure. This is helpful for quickly spotting misconfigurations or finding unhealthy targets.

sources.title: Getting started - Prometheus
sources.claim: Prometheus collects metrics from targets by scraping metrics HTTP endpoints.

sources.title: HTTP 503 (Service Unavailable): what it means and how to fix it | 3v-Hosting
sources.claim: Server overload - when your web server receives more requests than it can handle (perhaps due to a viral post, bot attack, or poorly optimized code), it may start rejecting new connections with a 503 response; Resource limitations - in virtual hosting or VPS environments, if your account exceeds CPU, memory, or process limits, the hosting system may return a 503 code until resources become available again.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.

Optional improvement: Clarify whether 'Prometheus entirely returning 503' always implies the UI is inaccessible, or if it refers to core metric collection/querying functionality being impaired while the UI remains partially available for diagnostics.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
Optional improvement: Soften absolute claims unless they are directly sourced.
